articles.title: Pentagon releases initial batch of declassified files detailing UFOs
articles.summary: The Pentagon released a batch of “never‑before‑seen” declassified files on UFOs after President Donald Trump directed the agency to do so earlier in the year. The files contain investigations of reported sightings that span decades, including a large FBI file with hundreds of pages of eyewitness testimonies and public reports from 1947 to 1968 and more recent military memos describing a possible small UAP in Iraq in 2022 and multiple glares or lights of unknown origin observed in Syria in 2024.

en.title: Pentagon releases initial batch of declassified files detailing UFOs
en.summary: The Pentagon released a batch of “never‑before‑seen” declassified files on UFOs after President Donald Trump directed the agency to do so earlier in the year. The files contain investigations of reported sightings that span decades, including a large FBI file with hundreds of pages of eyewitness testimonies and public reports from 1947 to 1968 and more recent military memos describing a possible small UAP in Iraq in 2022 and multiple glares or lights of unknown origin observed in Syria in 2024.

de.title: Das Pentagon veröffentlicht den ersten Stapel freigegebener UFO-Dateien
de.summary: Das Pentagon veröffentlichte eine Reihe „nie zuvor veröffentlichter“ freigegebener UFO-Dateien, nachdem Präsident Donald Trump die Behörde Anfang des Jahres dazu angewiesen hatte. Die Akten enthalten Untersuchungen zu gemeldeten Sichtungen, die sich über Jahrzehnte erstrecken, darunter eine große FBI-Akte mit Hunderten von Seiten an Augenzeugenaussagen und öffentlichen Berichten von 1947 bis 1968 sowie neuere militärische Memos, die eine mögliche kleine UAP im Irak im Jahr 2022 und mehrere in Syrien im Jahr 2024 beobachtete Blendungen oder Lichter unbekannter Herkunft beschreiben.

de.title: Laut CSEO-Chef markieren freigegebene UAP-Veröffentlichungen eine neue Phase der Offenlegung | Zypern informieren
de.summary: George Danos, Leiter der Cyprus Space Exploration Organization, sagte, die Veröffentlichung von freigegebenem UAP-Material markiert eine neue Phase der Offenlegung. Er sagte der Nachrichtenagentur Zypern, dass die kommende Zeit entscheidend dafür sein könnte, ob die Menschheit allein sei oder von fortgeschrittenen Zivilisationen besucht werde. Danos stellte fest, dass die Vereinigten Staaten im Rahmen einer Transparenzinitiative Dateien über nicht identifizierte anomale Phänomene freigegeben und veröffentlicht haben und dass mehrere freigegebene Videos bereits von Hunderten Millionen Menschen heruntergeladen wurden.

summaries.batch-declassified-detailing-files-initi: The Pentagon released a batch of “never‑before‑seen” declassified files on UFOs after President Donald Trump directed the agency to do so earlier in the year. The files contain investigations of reported sightings that span decades, including a large FBI file with hundreds of pages of eyewitness testimonies and public reports from 1947 to 1968 and more recent military memos describing a possible small UAP in Iraq in 2022 and multiple glares or lights of unknown origin observed in Syria in 2024. The Pentagon said the documents are now available to the public with no clearance required and will post new materials on a rolling basis every few weeks. A disclaimer on the Pentagon website notes that the descriptive language in the military memos reflects the subjective interpretation of the writer and should not be taken as a conclusive indication of what actually happened. The files also include reports from Apollo 12 astronauts in 1969 who saw flashes of light sailing off the Moon and from Apollo 17 astronauts in 1972 who saw bright particles of light that they theorized might be chunks of ice. Trump’s Truth Social post and NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman’s statement both praised the release as a step toward greater transparency about unidentified anomalous phenomena.
summaries.begins-can-files-make-minds-own-pentagon: The Pentagon has begun releasing new files on UFOs, stating that the public can draw their own conclusions about unidentified anomalous phenomena. The effort is led by the White House, the director of national intelligence, the Energy Department, NASA, and the FBI. In a post on X, the Pentagon said that past administrations had sought to discredit or dissuade the public, while President Donald Trump is focused on maximum transparency and allowing people to form their own opinions. The Pentagon will release additional documents on a rolling basis, and Trump has been teasing the announcement since February, having previously released records on the assassinations of President John F. Kennedy, Senator Robert F. Kennedy, and Martin Luther King Jr. that revealed little beyond what was already known. Congress created an office in 2022 to declassify UFO material, and its 2024 report found hundreds of new UAP incidents but no evidence that the U.S. government had confirmed alien technology. A small group of Republicans, including Rep. Anna Paulina Luna and Rep. Tim Burchett, have urged further transparency, with Luna demanding 46 UAP videos identified by whistleblowers and Burchett noting that transparency will take time; experts caution that UAP videos are often misinterpreted by those unfamiliar with advanced military technology.
summaries.disclosure-files-following-government-ja: The Japanese government is analyzing a trove of previously classified Pentagon files on unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAP) that were released by the United States, which include two videos of sightings near Japan. Chief Cabinet Secretary Minoru Kihara confirmed at a Tokyo press conference that the government is gathering and analyzing information on unidentified aerial objects on a daily basis while coordinating with the United States and other countries. Kihara said he personally viewed the footage after the U.S. Department of Defense released 162 files containing videos, photographs, and documents linked to unresolved aerial incidents. One nearly two‑minute 2023 video from the U.S. Indo‑Pacific Command shows three distinct areas of contrast maintaining fixed positions and orientations relative to one another near Japan, while a nine‑second 2024 video shows a football‑shaped UAP near the East China Sea. When asked about releasing its own UAP files, Kihara stated that the government would make case‑by‑case decisions after comprehensively considering factors such as the risk of exposing intelligence‑gathering capabilities. In 2024 a group of about 80 Japanese lawmakers was formed to investigate UAPs as a serious security issue, and in March that group urged the creation of a specialized UAP department under the deputy chief cabinet secretary for crisis management.
summaries.chaos-cia-corbell-dog-fear-jeremy-lazar-: Cybernews published an article on May 12, 2026 titled “Jeremy Corbell’s Sleeping Dog: UFO whistleblower chaos, CIA fear, and Lazar lore.” The piece discusses Jeremy Corbell’s involvement in UFO investigations and highlights alleged chaos surrounding a whistleblower. It also references fears expressed by the CIA regarding the subject matter. Additionally, the article touches on the lore surrounding the Lazar case. The article is part of a cluster of Cybernews sources covering UAP topics.
summaries.cseo-cyprus-declassified-disclosure-head: George Danos, head of the Cyprus Space Exploration Organisation, said the release of declassified UAP material marks a new phase of disclosure. He told the Cyprus News Agency that the coming period could be key to determining whether humanity is alone or visited by advanced civilizations. Danos noted that the United States has declassified and released files on unidentified anomalous phenomena as part of a transparency initiative, and that multiple declassified videos have already been downloaded by hundreds of millions of people. He said public interest has grown rapidly and officials are now more willing to discuss incidents openly, noting that pilots who previously did not report sightings now do so. Danos mentioned that some officials have suggested humanity may not be alone and that a total change in how the issue is handled is expected. He claimed that some released footage shows unusual flight dynamics that are unlikely to match known human technology.
summaries.briefed-claim-congregations-disclosure-i: Several religious leaders, including Pastor Perry Stone and evangelist Tony Merkel, claim they were privately briefed by U.S. military intelligence officers to prepare their congregations for an upcoming UAP disclosure. Stone, a Tennessee‑based Pentecostal evangelist, said in a video that a trusted associate informed him that a group of pastors was invited to hear directly from government‑connected individuals about an imminent disclosure event involving unidentified anomalous phenomena. Stone described the briefing as covering craft not allegedly part of our planet, non‑human beings, and a coordinated propaganda plan already in motion. Merkel says he has been in contact with officials for over a year, describing them as Christians in intelligence operations assigned to work within the UAP disclosure community, and that they approached him with a mission to prepare the body of Christ for what is coming. The state where the briefing took place has not been disclosed, and no government memo, official invitation, transcript, or attendee list has surfaced. The White House has not denied the claims, and Washington has remained silent, a silence the article notes is significant.
summaries.administration-america-file-gov-historic: On May 8 2026 the Department of War announced the first release of new, never‑before‑seen government files on Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena (UAP), also known as UFOs. The release is part of the Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters (PURSUE), an interagency effort that includes the White House, the Office of the Director of National Intelligence, the Department of Energy, AARO, NASA, the FBI, and other intelligence components. The files are housed at WAR.GOV/UFO and more releases are expected on a rolling basis. The initial tranche contains more than 160 documents, ranging from State Department and FBI reports to NASA mission transcripts, eyewitness accounts, photographs, and a 1947 Pan Am crew sighting of a bright orange object. The Department of War notes that the cases are unresolved, meaning the government has not made a definitive determination about the phenomena, and that unresolved status may result from limited data, incomplete information, or the need for additional technical analysis. The files do not constitute official confirmation of alien technology, recovered alien materials, or extraterrestrial life, and Secretary of War Pete Hegseth described the release as a transparency effort aligned with President Trump’s order.
summaries.1972-2026-declassified-images-landing-mo: The Pentagon has released 17 newly declassified images of unidentified aerial phenomena (UAP) that span from the 1972 Apollo 17 moon landing to 2026. The release was reported by MSN on May 14, 2026. The images were declassified by the Pentagon. The collection covers a period from 1972 to 2026. The disclosure is part of the Pentagon’s ongoing efforts to share UAP data.
summaries.340-first-hits-hours-million-pentagon-re: The Pentagon has reported that a website dedicated to unidentified flying objects received 340 million hits within its first 12 hours of launch. The claim was reported by NewsNation on May 9, 2026. The Pentagon’s statement underscores the significant public interest in UAPs. summaries.artemis-back-decades-deep-files-humanity: NASA has long projected that there is no confirmed evidence of alien spacecraft visiting Earth, a stance that remains unchanged in its official position. The Trump administration’s Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters (PURSUE) has been releasing previously hidden files that include military encounters, radar tracking incidents, astronaut observations, and classified investigations spanning decades. These newly released files show that numerous U.S. government agencies have treated the UFO phenomenon as serious enough to warrant classified investigations, intelligence monitoring, aerospace analysis, and national‑security scrutiny for decades. Historically, the public was led to believe UFO sightings were largely due to weather balloons, optical or atmospheric distortions, camera artifacts, or misidentifications, and NASA publicly explained many astronaut sightings as ice particles, reflected sunlight, orbital debris, or camera anomalies. The contrast now is that the federal system openly acknowledges unresolved encounters involving astronauts, military pilots, advanced radar systems, transmedium objects, and unidentified craft with capabilities not easily explained by conventional aerospace engineering. The contradiction highlighted by the article is not that NASA lied outright, but that its public tone of certainty and dismissal has shifted to a more cautious acknowledgment of unexplained phenomena.
summaries.files-info-interesting-never-pentagon-re: President Donald Trump announced that the Pentagon is preparing to release UFO files that his administration has uncovered, describing them as “very interesting” and suggesting that they will be unprecedented in the U.S. government’s history. In February, Trump directed federal agencies to release records on extraterrestrial life, unidentified aerial phenomena (UAP), and UFOs, and he has since teased an imminent release of documents that have never before been shared. The Pentagon has been declassifying UFO documents for years, and a 2024 report from the All‑Domain Anomaly Resolution Office identified hundreds of new UAP incidents but found no evidence that the U.S. government had confirmed alien technology. Former director Sean Kirkpatrick said that Trump’s promises are bluster and that there are no bombshell revelations, noting that the government’s records contain no photos or interviews with aliens. A GOP‑led Task Force on the Declassification of Federal Secrets, co‑chaired by Florida Representative Anna Paulina Luna, has demanded UAP videos from the Pentagon, but a deadline set by Luna passed without any videos being produced. Vice President JD Vance has described himself as “obsessed” with UFO files and believes that sightings are the work of spiritual demons, while Trump himself has expressed uncertainty about the existence of extraterrestrial life.
summaries.anomalous-department-effort-files-histor: On May 8 2026 the Department of War announced the initial release of new, never‑before‑seen files on Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena (UAP) as part of the Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters (PURSUE). The interagency effort includes the White House, the Office of the Director of National Intelligence, the Department of Energy, the War’s All‑Domain Anomaly Resolution Office, NASA, the FBI, and other U.S. intelligence components. The collection will be housed on WAR.GOV/UFO and additional files will be released on a rolling basis. Many of the materials have not yet been analyzed for resolution of anomalies, although all have been reviewed for security purposes. The FBI, NASA, and other officials have publicly affirmed the commitment to transparency while emphasizing that national security remains a priority.
summaries.bright-but-files-hot-interpretation-leav: The Pentagon released a new batch of declassified UFO files on Friday, including videos, images, and transcripts from the State Department, FBI, and NASA, after President Trump announced a major document dump on Truth Social. Other documents include a 1969 Apollo 11 debrief where Buzz Aldrin noted a bright light that might have been a laser, a 2023 FBI interview of a drone pilot who saw a linear object with visible bands, and a 1972 Apollo 17 NASA photo of three dots in a triangular formation. The Pentagon states that many of the cases remain unresolved and that preliminary analysis suggests some objects could be physical, but no consensus exists on their nature. The 2024 Pentagon report found no evidence that the U.S. government has recovered alien technology or confirmed alien life, and the release is part of an ongoing effort to declassify UAP material that began in 2022.

summaries.agents-beat-drop-eye-federal-first-off-p: On May 8 2026 the U.S. Department of War posted a web page titled PURSUE that contains 161 declassified UAP records, the first tranche released under a February 19 Trump administration directive to comb federal archives for UAP material. The only file with a narrative title is a 13‑page slide deck called “Western US Event,” which summarizes statements from seven federal law‑enforcement special agents (USPER1–USPER7) who reported four distinct sightings in the western United States during 2023. The four sub‑events described are: “Orbs Launching Orbs,” where an orange orb released two to four red orbs; “Large, Fiery Orb,” a stationary glowing orange orb about 12–18 m across and 1,050 m away; “Dark Kite,” a low‑flying, car‑like object that glided over the desert; and “Transparent Kite,” a kite‑shaped form that appeared to block a spotlight beam. The report notes that the agents’ accounts are corroborated by a teammate using night‑vision goggles, that AARO later revised the size of the Large, Fiery Orb, and that no technical data such as radar tracks or sensor video accompany the Transparent Kite event. The remaining 160 records include roughly 50 Department of War mission reports from 2020–2024, 24 FBI photo PDFs dated “Late 2025,” 16 sections of an FBI “Flying Discs” administrative file, six NASA Apollo transcripts and images, about 25 unresolved UAP summary reports, five State Department UAP cables, and the Western US Event slide deck. The Department’s statement, attributed to Secretary Pete Hegseth, calls the release “unprecedented transparency,” and the PURSUE landing page features a real‑time clock, coordinates set to the Pentagon, and an all‑caps exhortation from President Trump on Truth Social.
summaries.access-apollo-astronauts-declassified-fi: The Department of War, under President Donald Trump and with support from the Director of National Intelligence, released a set of UFO files to the public on Friday, citing a desire for “complete and maximum transparency.” Trump posted on Truth Social that previous administrations had not been open on the issue and that the release was part of his promise to share information on unidentified aerial phenomena. The Pentagon has published about 161 declassified files, including FBI memoranda, Air Force intelligence reports, military footage, infrared imagery, NASA transcripts, and a 1948 Air Force report on “flying saucers” near sensitive military sites in Europe. Apollo astronaut transcripts in the archive record observations of bright lights by Buzz Aldrin in 1969, particles and flashes by Alan Bean, and Jack Schmitt’s description of lights as “like the Fourth of July,” though some reports suggest the lights may have been reflections. Military sensors captured several UFO sightings, such as an eight‑pointed star object, a football‑shaped craft, a super‑hot orb that jammed sensors in Africa, a diamond‑shaped UAP traveling at roughly 499 mph in a 2024 video, and a luminous sphere moving between wind turbines. The Department of War has stated it will release additional declassified videos and high‑resolution photos in the coming weeks, but officials note that most cases remain unresolved due to insufficient data rather than confirmed extraterrestrial origin.

summaries.2026-dump-files-gain-intrigue-pentagons-: The Pentagon released a batch of previously classified files on unidentified flying objects, including Buzz Aldrin’s description of a “fairly bright light” aboard Apollo, a mysterious object that made multiple 90‑degree turns over the Aegean Sea, and a bright object that performed corkscrew twists over Kazakhstan. California UFO sightings reported to the National UFO Reporting Center in 2026 include cigar‑shaped objects, rotating flat panels, fireballs that change direction, and whirling pink and white orbs, with specific incidents such as a Lake Elsinore meteor‑like object that floated and vanished, a light hovering above Fresno Zoo exhibits, and a Mendocino couple witnessing pulsating colored lights over the Pacific. Additional reports from April and May describe giant orange orbs off the Pacific coast and a fireball‑type object with a clear fire trail that changed direction, respectively. The Pentagon’s release follows a 2022 congressional mandate to declassify UFO documents, and a 2024 report found no evidence of alien technology, though some Republican lawmakers claim the agency is withholding information. Experts caution that UAP videos are often misinterpreted and that the new documents, described as “never‑before‑seen” though some were previously public, may fuel speculation rather than provide clarity. The files include videos, imagery, and testimony such as a U.S. intelligence official’s encounter with a “super‑hot” orb, an FBI interview with a drone pilot who saw a glowing linear object, a 1972 NASA photo of three dots in triangular formation, and numerous military videos from Syria, Japan, and North America. Sean Kirkpatrick, former head of the Pentagon’s All‑Domain Anomaly Resolution Office, warned that without analysis the release will likely increase conspiracy theories and pseudoscience.

summaries.big-festival-oregon-puts-small-town-visi: The 26th Annual McMenamins UFO Festival in McMinnville, Oregon, runs from May 14 to May 17 and is hosted by the McMenamins Hotel Oregon, making it the second‑largest UFO festival in the United States after Roswell. summaries.encounters-presidential-pursue-reporting: The Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters (PURSUE) was created in response to a directive from President Donald J. Trump to increase transparency about unidentified aerial phenomena (UAP) and related government records. The Department of War, with support from the Office of the Director of National Intelligence, is coordinating a nationwide effort to locate, review, declassify, and publicly release unresolved UAP‑related documents held by federal agencies. The initiative involves dozens of agencies and the examination of tens of millions of records, many of which exist only on paper and span several decades. The Department of War will publish new materials on a rolling basis, releasing tranches of declassified documents every few weeks as they are discovered. The released files are classified as unresolved cases, meaning the government cannot definitively determine the nature of the observed phenomena due to insufficient data. The Department of War also plans to continue separate reporting on resolved UAP cases as required by statute. Release 01 of the PURSUE archive was cleared for release on May 8, 2026, and includes a statement from Secretary of War Pete Hegseth affirming the administration’s commitment to unprecedented transparency.
